perturbation.original_text: 1.31 "Product" or "Products" means a product or products set forth in Exhibit A for Marketing by or for ETON in the Territory (and covered or intended to be covered by a Dossier) and manufactured and supplied by Aucta (or a Third Party as permitted by this Agreement) to ETON in fully packaged and labeled form and ready for commercialization by ETON.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.31 "Product" or "Products" means a product or products set forth in Exhibit A for Marketing by or for ETON in the Territory (and covered or intended to be covered by a Dossier) and manufactured and supplied by Aucta (or a Third Party as permitted by this Agreement) to ETON in fully packaged and labeled form and ready for commercialization by Aucta.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding who is responsible for commercialization of the Product. Originally, the definition states that the product is 'ready for commercialization by ETON,' which aligns with ETON's responsibilities for Marketing as defined in Section 5.1. The modified text stating 'ready for commercialization by Aucta,' contradicts ETON's granted exclusive right, creating uncertainty. This contradicts the responsibilities assigned to ETON, raising concerns about whether ETON has the exclusive right to commercialize the product. The section that is being contradicted is Section 5.1, which assignates the full responsibility for the Marketing of the product, and it clearly gives ETON sole and exclusive right.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. SALES, MARKETING AND DISTRIBUTION 5.1 ETON shall be solely responsible for the Marketing of the Products and shall have sole and exclusive right to make all Marketing decisions for the Product in the Territory, including without limitation to pricing, contracting, sub-licensing, co-promoting, or any contract promotion activities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts section 5.1. The original text states that the product is ready for commercialization by ETON, which is consistent with ETON's sole responsibility for marketing. However, the changed text asserts that the product is ready for commercialization by Aucta. This directly conflicts with Section 5.1, which grants ETON the sole and exclusive right to make all Marketing decisions for the Product. Aucta commercializing the product directly impedes ETON's exclusive right.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 Term. The term of this Agreement shall automatically become effective upon the occurrence of (i) ETON executing a commercial supply agreement with a contract manufacturing organization within forty-five (45) days of the Execution Date, provided that ETON has exercised best efforts to execute such agreement and the failure to execute is solely caused by the refusal or inability of the proposed manufacturing organization to sign a reasonable agreement; and (ii) acceptance for review of the Dossier or marketing application for [ * * * ] by the FDA no later than September 2, 2019 (such date, the "Effective Date") and shall end upon the termination or expiration of the Agreement as set forth in Section 11 (the "Term"). For avoidance of doubt, all rights conferred to ETON under this Agreement for the purpose of allowing ETON to Market the Product in the Territory shall continue until a Party terminates this Agreement. Aucta should continue to receive 15% of Net Sales Royalty for as long as ETON is selling the Product(s) in the Territory, unless otherwise agreed to under this Agreement. The obligations of ETON to consummate the transactions contemplated by this Agreement shall be subject to the fulfillment or ETON's waiver of the occurrence of the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 Term. The term of this Agreement shall automatically become effective upon the occurrence of (i) ETON executing a commercial supply agreement with a contract manufacturing organization within forty-five (45) days of the Execution Date, provided that ETON has exercised best efforts to execute such agreement and the failure to execute is solely caused by the refusal or inability of the proposed manufacturing organization to sign a reasonable agreement; and (ii) acceptance for review of the Dossier or marketing application for [ * * * ] by the FDA no later than September 2, 2019 (such date, the "Effective Date") and shall end after 10 years from the Effective Date (the "Term"). For avoidance of doubt, all rights conferred to ETON under this Agreement for the purpose of allowing ETON to Market the Product in the Territory shall continue until a Party terminates this Agreement. Aucta should continue to receive 15% of Net Sales Royalty for as long as ETON is selling the Product(s) in the Territory, unless otherwise agreed to under this Agreement. The obligations of ETON to consummate the transactions contemplated by this Agreement shall be subject to the fulfillment or ETON's waiver of the occurrence of the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction on when the Term of the agreement shall end. Originally the Term ends upon the termination or expiration of the agreement as stated in Section 11, meaning termination conditions in Section 11 determine how long it lasts. The modified definition defines that the Term shall end after 10 years from the Effective date. This creates uncertainty, considering the fact that another condition for royalty payment is for as long as ETON is selling the Product(s) in the Territory. In Section 6.3.1, it creates a contradiction if ETON continues to sell the product in the territory after those 10 years. This means that if the contract continues after 10 years because ETON keeps selling the product, that royalty payments can become a subject of litigation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3. Royalty. 6.3.1 ETON shall pay to Aucta a royalty payment of [ * * * ] of Net Sales of the Products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11.1 introduces a fixed term of 10 years, which directly contradicts the original condition that the agreement ends upon termination or expiration as defined in Section 11 and the royalty payment condition in Section 6.3.1, which continues as long as ETON is selling the product. If ETON continues selling the product after the 10-year term defined in the modified 11.1, a conflict arises with Section 6.3.1 regarding royalty payments, creating a significant in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Development and Registration Responsibilities. 3.1.1 At its sole cost and expense, Aucta shall be responsible and liable for all development and manufacturing activities required for the filing and approval of the Dossiers for the Products in and for the Territory, including without limitation all costs and management of any required pre-approval and post-approval clinical or other studies.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Development and Registration Responsibilities. 3.1.1 At its sole cost and expense, Eton shall be responsible and liable for all development and manufacturing activities required for the filing and approval of the Dossiers for the Products in and for the Territory, including without limitation all costs and management of any required pre-approval and post-approval clinical or other studies.
perturbation.explanation: The change assigns the responsibilities of development and manufacturing to ETON, which directly contradicts the original agreement that Aucta is liable for these costs. This perturbation creates ambiguity in multiple sections. The immediate contradiction occurs within section 3.2 Registration Maintenance and Regulatory Responsibilities, where Aucta continues to hold the responsibilities associated with Dossiers approval and maintenance, so if Eton makes the Dossier and activities towards that, why Aucta should be responsible for their maintenance. This affects financial responsibilities (since costs are now ambiguously assigned).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Registration Maintenance and Regulatory Responsibilities. 3.2.1 Aucta shall hold the approved Dossiers in its name and be responsible for their maintenance. Aucta will take all actions with the FDA, including paying all fees and conducting all communications with FDA or other Governmental Entities as required by Applicable Law in respect of the Dossiers, including without limitation payment of fees owed under the Prescription Drug User Fee Act, Annual Branded Prescription Drug Fees assessed under Section 9008 of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (ACA), Public Law 111-148 (124 Stat. 119 (2010)), as amended by Section 1404 of the Health Care and Education Reconciliation Act of 2010 (HCERA), Public Law 111-152 (124 Stat. 1029 (2010)), or any successor laws, and preparing and filing all required reports (including adverse drug experience reports) with the appropriate Governmental Entity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3.1.1 assigns development and manufacturing responsibilities to Eton, while the contradicted text in Section 3.2.1 states that Aucta holds the approved Dossiers and is responsible for their maintenance. This creates a direct conflict because if Eton is responsible for the Dossiers' creation, Aucta's responsibility for their maintenance becomes ambiguous and contradictory, creating an ambiguity as to who is liable for costs associated with each.
